headline: Survey at 36% target with strong regional spread; teacher voice dominates data quality.
collection_status: 481 of 1,350 responses collected (35.6% completion). All 10 regions represented with balanced distribution; Lindi, Mara, and Tanga account for 42% of sample. Pace suggests on track for target if current collection rate maintained through Q2 2026.
key_findings: Teacher-led feedback (260/481 = 54%): Lower KF teachers (195) are primary respondents, complemented by head teachers (115). Leadership voices (WEO, DEO, RC, REO, ARC) represent only 8% of responses—significant blind spot for district/regional implementation insights.

Expansion appetite outpaces capacity: 175 respondents (36%) prioritise expanding KF vs. only 76 (16%) calling to strengthen existing systems. Scale readiness score of 2.8/5 signals implementation risk if expansion outpaces operational consolidation.

Partner satisfaction high (4.4/5) despite readiness gap: Stakeholders are engaged and supportive, but qualitative feedback emphasises need for digital teacher tracking, regular feedback loops, and government policy integration—not currently reflected in priority rankings.

Feedback quality concern: 5 non-KF teachers and 4 unspecified 'other officials' in sample. Qualitative responses show Swahili-language challenges in data capture (partial/truncated entries), risking analysis validity.
flags: District-level voice missing: Only 7 DEO + 4 district officials in 481 responses. Critical for understanding policy bottlenecks and scaling constraints—recommend targeted DEO outreach in remaining 869 responses.
Scale readiness paradox: 2.8/5 score on readiness to scale contradicts 36% expansion priority. Risk of misaligned expectations between field and leadership on implementation capacity.

Data quality drift: Truncated Swahili feedback and unspecified respondent categories suggest data collection tool or enumerator calibration issues. Validate remaining survey instrument before field completion.
recommendation: Immediately boost DEO/district-level sampling to 15% of remaining responses (130+ officials) to close implementation insight gap. Simultaneously audit data collection protocols in lower-performing regions (Rukwa 4%, Singida 6%) to ensure quality. Conduct rapid sense-check on scale readiness drivers before Q3 planning.
